text:
Bisky × Killua × New Special Attack (ビスケ×キルア×新必殺技, Bisuke × Kirua × Shin Hissatsuwaza) is the 89 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 11 th episode of the Greed Island final OVA series. It was released on DVD on June 30 th , 2004.

Overview

Gon proposed a deal to Genthru: the loser of their fight would surrender all their cards to the winner, prompting the two to engage in battle. Meanwhile, Biscuit initiated the plan by separating Bara from his comrades, allowing her to transform into her true form and easily defeat her opponent. Killua employed a similar tactic, successfully overcoming Bara with his new weapon. Gon's unwavering determination to persist compelled Genthru to alter his strategy, ultimately leading him to use his Nen ability during the fight.

Synopsis

In a tense confrontation, Genthru demands answers from Gon about his awareness of his abilities. In response, Gon summons his Book and boldly confronts Genthru, expressing his disdain for Genthru's forceful tactics in taking what he wants. Gon proposes a high-stakes deal: whoever surrenders first must hand over all their cards to the victor. Genthru, unfazed, reminds Gon that he is in no position to negotiate. However, Gon firmly declares that he will not willingly give up his cards, even at the cost of his life. As the two engage in a fierce stare-down, Genthru senses Gon's resolve and acknowledges his intelligence, albeit differently. He fully accepts the deal, summoning his Book to reveal the types and number of cards he possesses. Gon follows suit, and the two begin to charge their aura.

As Genthru gauges Gon's aura, he becomes aware of Gon's inexperience after Gon launches an attack, recalling Biscuit's lesson that the best defense is a good offense. Frustrated by Genthru's overwhelming display of aura, Gon charges at him, unleashing a relentless barrage of Nen attacks. While Genthru acknowledges Gon's skill, he points out Gon's deficiency in managing his aura, suggesting that his attacking speed outpaces his ability to maintain it. Genthru quietly derides Gon for his inexperience in combat.

Estimating the situation, Gon, despite knowing that conventional attacks won't affect Genthru, continues his assault. Genthru retaliates, delivering a punch that sends Gon crashing to the ground. Pausing momentarily, Genthru asks Gon again from whom he learned his abilities. Receiving no response, Genthru confidently declares that due to their difference in strength, he won't even need to use his Nen abilities against him. Nevertheless, Gon remains resolute despite adversities and is determined to force Genthru to use his Nen to initiate the plan. Genthru surges forward, landing a relentless series of blows on Gon, each strike hitting with precision and force. Gon's body is battered as he's sent crashing into a nearby tree, the impact driving him to his knees. Yet, even in the face of such punishment, Gon maintains focus. His eyes locked into Genthru. Genthru recognizes Gon's intent and forces him to use his Nen Ability. However, Genthru remains composed, determined to preserve his Nen ability. With a smug grin, he steps forward and launches another brutal barrage of attacks. Despite the relentless barrage of blows, Gon remains unfazed and remains focused. Genthru's frustration deepens with each failed attempt to rattle him. Anger flickers in his eyes as he charges forward yet again, determined to break Gon's composure. With a surge of force, he delivers a powerful punch.

Meanwhile, Biscuit kept fleeing from Bara, who was quickly closing the distance between them. Just as Bara was about to catch up, he was taken aback when Biscuit unexpectedly summoned her binder, assuming she had no more traversal cards left. His surprise turned to shock when she instantly activated Accompany, transporting both of them to Soufrabi. Bara stood dumbfounded, wondering why Biscuit had brought him to a place without allies. In response, Biscuit calmly explained that the truth was quite the opposite; she had brought him here to isolate him, ensuring he couldn't call for help. Biscuit made it clear she was stronger than him and then charged at Bara, unleashing a relentless series of attacks that struck him repeatedly. After a short while, Bara calmly informed her that her blows didn't hurt. Biscuit acknowledged his strength, but as she prepared to attack again, Bara anticipated her move and countered with a powerful slap that sent her crashing to the ground. After exchanging a few words, Biscuit became muscular, leaving Bara in shock. In this form, Biscuit warned him that she usually killed her opponents. She struck Bara in the face with a single punch, sending him reeling and crashing to the ground. Unable to fight any longer, Bara asked why Biscuit hid her true strength. She replied that she only used her trump card at the last moment and that she actually despised this form. As her words hung in the air, Bara fell unconscious.

Killua, employing the same tactic as Biscuit, faced off against Sub, who posed the same question. Killua remained silent and then carefully analyzed his opponent, confirming that he was slightly physically stronger than Sub. However, he sensed that he was likely at a disadvantage regarding aura. As Killua continued to gauge his opponent, he made his move, fully aware that his normal attacks wouldn't have any significant effect. He delivered a kick that landed with little to no impact. In response, Sub unleashed a flurry of attacks, which Killua skillfully dodged. However, Sub eventually landed a hit, and Killua felt the pain resonate in his body and striking his head. Seizing this opportunity, Killua experimented with his opponent, using his agility to keep Sub on his toes. Suddenly, multiple clones appeared, forcing Sub into an offensive position. Unfortunately for Sub, he struck one of the clones, giving Killua the opportunity he needed. In a swift move, Killua touched Sub and released a jolt of electric static, freezing him in place for a brief moment. Sub regained his composure, suspecting that Killua was concealing a weapon capable of generating electricity. With this newfound insight into Sub's durability against his electric attacks, Killua revealed a yo-yo, instantly alerting Sub. He swung the yoyo directly at Sub, who deftly evaded the strike, causing the yo-yo to hit a nearby tree and rip a large chunk from its trunk, ultimately causing the tree to topple over.

Astonished, Sub wondered what Killua's yo-yo was made of. Killua gladly replied that it was constructed from a special metal weighing 50 kg, warning Sub that getting hit by it would surely hurt. He then launched another swipe, and this time, Sub felt the impact, realizing just how dangerous Killua's yo-yo could be. Kneeling on the ground, Sub devised a strategy to neutralize the yo-yo while looking for an opportunity to strike at Killua's vulnerabilities. As Killua prepared his next attack, Sub moved, hurling a stone directly at Killua's upper arm, which dislodged the yo-yo from his grasp. This gave Sub the chance to land a hit on Killua.

After the successful strike, Sub continued talking, but was abruptly struck by Killua's yo-yo, which came out of nowhere and hit him in the back of the head, revealing that Killua had two yo-yos. The blow caused Sub to fall face-first to the ground, leaving him feeling nauseous.

Killua, aware of Sub's intentions, elaborated on why he had separated him from the others, revealing that it was all part of a setup. He then delivered another blow to Sub, sending him crashing down once more, and followed it up with a jolt of electricity that ended the fight decisively.

Back with Gon, Genthru maintained the upper hand. Gon appeared worse for wear, having absorbed more of Genthru's attacks, yet he remained stubborn and refused to give up. This prompted Genthru to alter his strategy, opting to use his Nen ability to not only defeat Gon but also shatter his spirit, believing that was the only way to force Gon to admit defeat.

Gon adopted a defensive stance, his aura flowing around him, while Genthru took on an offensive posture, his aura surging through his body. Suddenly, Genthru closed in on Gon, who could not react quickly. Genthru managed to seize Gon's hands, leaving him momentarily surprised.
